steps.title: Follow the legacy shift
steps.content: The first sentence gives the familiar perception of Nixon: he is most famous for Watergate, a political scandal involving criminality and eroded ethics. The second sentence begins with "But," which signals a shift away from that single negative view. It then adds facts about tribal sovereignty and health care reform to show that Nixon's legacy is more complex than the Watergate-focused perception.

steps.title: Reject inaccurate structures
steps.content:
• Choice A wrongly treats the first part as an achievement and says historians disregard it, neither of which matches the text.
  • Choice C invents a comparison among historical figures, but the passage is about Nixon's legacy.
  • Choice D says the text gives evidence both for and against an assertion, but the passage instead presents a perception and then complicates it with additional facts.
The remaining option describes that shift from a familiar perception to facts showing oversimplification. The answer is B.
